subtitle: A golden beach built in 1973 on a black volcanic coast, from Saharan sand and an Atlantic breakwater.
spike_statement: Roughly 270,000 tons of pale Saharan sand — some 5 million sacks shipped from El Aaiún aboard the Gopegui — were laid over Tenerife's black volcanic shore in 1973, behind a one-kilometer breakwater.

levers.photo_tone: Atlantic blue and Saharan gold set against the dark volcanic green of the Anaga massif behind
